concepts.name: Ashtekar-Barbero variables
concepts.definition: Canonical reformulation of general relativity in terms of an SU(2) connection A_a^i = Gamma_a^i + gamma K_a^i and a densitized triad E^a_i, recasting gravity as a gauge theory with a Yang-Mills-like phase space. They make the constraints polynomial and form the starting point for canonical LQG.

concepts.name: Background independence
concepts.definition: The requirement that the theory contains no fixed prior spacetime metric; geometry itself is fully dynamical and quantized. LQG implements this exactly, unlike perturbative/string approaches built on a background metric.

concepts.name: Diffeomorphism invariance
concepts.definition: Invariance under arbitrary smooth coordinate transformations (active diffeomorphisms), the gauge symmetry of general relativity. In LQG it forces a constraint algebra with structure functions and leads to spatially diff-invariant (knot) states.

concepts.name: Holonomy-flux algebra
concepts.definition: The fundamental kinematical algebra of LQG built from holonomies of the connection along edges and fluxes of the triad through surfaces, replacing pointwise fields. By the LOST/Fleischhack theorem it admits a unique diffeomorphism-invariant cyclic representation.

concepts.name: LOST/Fleischhack uniqueness theorem
concepts.definition: Theorem (Lewandowski-Okolow-Sahlmann-Thiemann; Fleischhack) that the holonomy-flux algebra has a unique diffeomorphism-invariant state, hence a unique kinematical representation (the Ashtekar-Lewandowski representation). It gives LQG kinematic rigidity.

concepts.name: Spin network
concepts.definition: A graph embedded in a spatial slice with edges labeled by SU(2) irreps (spins j) and vertices by intertwiners; spin networks form an orthonormal basis of the kinematical Hilbert space. Edges carry quanta of area and vertices quanta of volume.

concepts.name: Discrete area spectrum
concepts.definition: The LQG area operator has a discrete spectrum A = 8 pi gamma l_P^2 sum sqrt(j(j+1)), so surfaces have a minimum nonzero quantum of area (area gap). A signature prediction of quantized geometry.

concepts.name: Discrete volume spectrum
concepts.definition: The LQG volume operator has a discrete spectrum with contributions from spin-network vertices of valence >= 4 with non-coplanar geometry, giving space a minimum meaningful volume. No closed-form general spectrum exists.

concepts.name: Quantum geometry
concepts.definition: The picture, emerging from discrete area/volume spectra, in which space has an atomic, granular structure at the Planck scale, with spin networks describing quantum states of 3-geometry.

concepts.name: Fundamental spacetime discreteness
concepts.definition: In LQG and spin foams the discreteness of geometry is taken as physical/fundamental, in contrast to lattice regularizations (CDT) where discreteness is removed by a continuum limit.

concepts.name: Barbero-Immirzi parameter
concepts.definition: A dimensionless free parameter gamma entering the Ashtekar-Barbero connection. Classically a canonical-transformation label (does not affect classical dynamics), but quantum-mechanically it sets the scale of discrete geometry and black hole entropy; fixed by matching S = A/4.

concepts.name: Spin foam
concepts.definition: A 2-complex (vertices, edges, faces) labeled by spins and intertwiners representing a history of spin networks; its amplitude is a product of face, edge and vertex amplitudes summed over labels, defining covariant LQG dynamics as a sum over geometries.

concepts.name: EPRL-FK spin foam model
concepts.definition: The modern 4D Lorentzian spin foam model (Engle-Pereira-Rovelli-Livine / Freidel-Krasnov) obtained by imposing linear simplicity constraints p = gamma j, k = j on BF theory via the Y-map embedding SU(2) irreps into SL(2,C). Reproduces the Regge action in the large-spin limit.

concepts.name: BF theory / Plebanski formulation
concepts.definition: A topological field theory (action B wedge F) from which gravity is recovered by imposing simplicity constraints forcing B = *(e wedge e). Spin foams realize this 'constrained BF' strategy by discretizing the path integral.

concepts.name: Y-map / linear simplicity constraint
concepts.definition: The map Y_gamma embedding SU(2) spin-j states into the SL(2,C) principal series (p = gamma j, k = j), implementing the simplicity (Plebanski) constraint at the quantum level and linking the canonical (SU(2)) and covariant (Lorentzian) theories.

concepts.name: Spin foam partition function / transition amplitude
concepts.definition: Z = sum over labelings of products of face, edge and vertex amplitudes on a 2-complex, providing the covariant dynamics (a sum over geometries) and ideally a projector onto the physical Hilbert space of canonical LQG.

concepts.name: Regge action (semiclassical limit)
concepts.definition: The discrete Einstein-Hilbert action on a triangulation; the large-spin asymptotics of the EPRL vertex amplitude is proportional to exp(i S_Regge), the key test of the spin-foam semiclassical limit.

concepts.name: Flatness problem of spin foams
concepts.definition: A pathology whereby the semiclassical (large-spin) limit of spin-foam amplitudes can be dominated by flat geometries, failing to reproduce full curved Regge dynamics; numerically confirmed in BF and EPRL cases.

concepts.name: Effective spin foam models
concepts.definition: Computationally efficient spin-foam models (Asante-Dittrich-Haggard) using the quantum area spectrum, strongly imposed gluing constraints and the discrete GR action; they avoid the flatness problem in a restricted parameter regime and enable first semiclassical tests.

concepts.name: Numerical spin foams (sl2cfoam-next)
concepts.definition: High-performance computation of Lorentzian EPRL amplitudes (sl2cfoam-next code) plus Monte Carlo sampling, enabling tests of single-vertex asymptotics, the flatness problem and the first explicit spin-foam dynamics with an internal edge.

concepts.name: Spin foam coarse-graining and renormalization
concepts.definition: Tensor-network and renormalization-group methods applied to spin foams to study refinement/continuum limits and phase structure, since Monte Carlo is obstructed by complex oscillating amplitudes.

concepts.name: Continuum limit of spin foams
concepts.definition: The central open problem of recovering smooth spacetime from spin foams. Recent TQFT/Refined-Algebraic-Quantisation analysis shows strong convergence forces a topological theory, motivating a distributional (rigging-map) construction of the physical Hilbert space.

concepts.name: Hamiltonian constraint
concepts.definition: The constraint encoding time evolution / dynamics in canonical LQG. Thiemann's quantization is well-defined and anomaly-free but graph-changing and ambiguous; the constraint algebra closes only with structure functions, not constants.

concepts.name: Master constraint programme
concepts.definition: Thiemann's proposal to combine all (infinitely many) Hamiltonian constraints into a single positive 'master' constraint M = integral H^2/sqrt(det q), bypassing the non-closure of the constraint algebra with structure functions.

concepts.name: Graph-changing dynamics
concepts.definition: Dynamics in which the Hamiltonian constraint changes both spin labels and the topology of the spin-network graph. First numerically implemented in 2024, revealing solutions and observables inaccessible to fixed-graph truncations.

concepts.name: Problem of time
concepts.definition: In a background-independent, fully constrained theory there is no external time; physical states are annihilated by the Hamiltonian constraint and evolution must be relational. A conceptual obstacle shared across canonical quantum gravity.

concepts.name: Isolated horizon
concepts.definition: A quasi-local definition of a black-hole horizon in equilibrium (no global stationarity required) carrying an SU(2) Chern-Simons theory. Horizon microstates are punctures where spin-network edges pierce the horizon.

concepts.name: SU(2) Chern-Simons horizon theory
concepts.definition: The boundary theory induced on an isolated horizon; counting its Hilbert-space dimension on a punctured sphere yields the black hole microstate count and entropy in the SU(2)-invariant formulation.

concepts.name: Black hole entropy from microstate counting
concepts.definition: LQG computes black hole entropy by counting horizon quantum-geometry microstates; matching the leading area term to S = A/4 fixes gamma, with a universal logarithmic correction -1/2 ln(A).

concepts.name: Bekenstein-Hawking entropy
concepts.definition: The semiclassical black hole entropy S = A/(4 l_P^2). LQG reproduces it from first principles by counting quantum-geometry microstates, a key success of the program.

concepts.name: Big bounce (LQC)
concepts.definition: In loop quantum cosmology the big bang singularity is replaced by a quantum bounce at critical density rho_c ~ 0.41 rho_Planck, where quantum geometry generates an effective repulsion connecting a contracting to an expanding phase.

concepts.name: Loop quantum cosmology (LQC)
concepts.definition: Symmetry-reduced (homogeneous) quantization using LQG techniques. Its effective Friedmann equation has a (1 - rho/rho_c) correction producing the big bounce and predictions for the CMB.

concepts.name: Singularity resolution
concepts.definition: The replacement of classical curvature singularities (big bang, black hole interior) by non-singular quantum bounces in LQC and effective LQG black-hole models.

concepts.name: Planck star
concepts.definition: A compact high-density remnant formed when gravitational collapse bounces at Planck density inside a black hole; after evaporation a quasi-stable Planck-mass remnant may remain, proposed as a dark-matter candidate and partial resolution of the information paradox.

concepts.name: Twisted geometries / twistors
concepts.definition: Classical interpretation of the LQG phase space on a fixed graph: each edge carries two twistors, with areas and Lorentzian dihedral angles as canonical pairs; holonomies and fluxes are spinor bilinears subject to area-matching and simplicity constraints.

concepts.name: Graviton propagator from spin foams
concepts.definition: Two-point (and higher n-point) correlation functions computed from spin foam amplitudes; the low-energy limit of the improved-model two-point function matches the graviton propagator of linearized gravity in a transverse radial gauge.

concepts.name: Semiclassical limit problem
concepts.definition: The unproven recovery of general relativity as the low-energy effective theory of LQG/spin foams. Large-spin and continuum limits need not commute; graph-changing Hamiltonian dynamics complicates coherent-state analysis.

concepts.name: Deformed Lorentz symmetry / DSR
concepts.definition: Linearization of the LQG deformed hypersurface-deformation algebra connects to kappa-Poincare symmetry and Doubly Special Relativity; Lorentz invariance is deformed (with an invariant Planck scale) rather than broken.

formulas.name: Ashtekar-Barbero Poisson bracket
formulas.meaning: Fundamental phase-space structure of canonical LQG: the SU(2) connection A and densitized triad E are conjugate, with the Barbero-Immirzi parameter gamma rescaling the coupling.

formulas.name: Ashtekar-Barbero connection
formulas.meaning: Combines spin connection Gamma and extrinsic curvature K into a single SU(2) connection; gamma = +-i gives the self-dual (polynomial-constraint) Ashtekar connection.

formulas.name: Holonomy of the connection
formulas.meaning: Path-ordered exponential of the connection along an edge; the smeared variable on which the holonomy-flux algebra and spin networks are built.

formulas.name: Area operator spectrum
formulas.meaning: Area is quantized; each spin-network edge piercing the surface contributes 8 pi gamma l_P^2 sqrt(j(j+1)). Minimum nonzero area (gap) for j=1/2 demonstrates atomic geometry.

formulas.name: Volume operator
formulas.meaning: Volume receives discrete contributions from spin-network vertices of valence >= 4; gives space a minimum meaningful volume.

formulas.name: Spin foam partition function
formulas.meaning: Covariant LQG dynamics as a sum over spin/intertwiner labelings of a 2-complex; the vertex amplitude A_v carries the physical content of the model.

formulas.name: EPRL Y-map / linear simplicity
formulas.latex: Y_\gamma: |j, m\rangle_{SU(2)} \longmapsto |(p=\gamma j,\, k=j);\, j, m\rangle_{SL(2,\mathbb{C})}
formulas.meaning: Embeds SU(2) spin states into the SL(2,C) principal series with p = gamma j, k = j, imposing the simplicity constraint and linking canonical and covariant LQG.

formulas.name: Vertex amplitude large-spin asymptotics
formulas.meaning: The EPRL vertex reproduces the Regge action semiclassically; the presence of multiple terms and degenerate configurations is the core semiclassical-limit difficulty.

formulas.name: Black hole entropy and gamma fixing
formulas.meaning: Microstate counting gives S proportional to area; matching to S=A/4 fixes gamma = gamma0. The full SU(2) counting gives gamma0 ~ 0.274; the older U(1)/Domagala-Lewandowski-Meissner counting gives gamma0 ~ 0.2375. The naive single-spin closed form ln2/(pi sqrt3) ~ 0.127 is NOT equal to the actual SU(2) value 0.274. The log-correction coefficient is convention/scheme dependent (-1/2 for U(1) area-counting; -3/2 in the SU(2) Chern-Simons treatment).

formulas.name: LQC effective Friedmann equation
formulas.meaning: The (1 - rho/rho_c) correction makes H vanish at rho=rho_c, producing the big bounce that replaces the big bang singularity.

formulas.name: Master constraint
formulas.meaning: Combines infinitely many Hamiltonian constraints into a single positive constraint, bypassing the non-closure of the algebra with structure functions; its kernel defines physical states.

formulas.name: Cosmological constant via quantum group
formulas.meaning: Deforming SU(2) to the quantum group SU(2)_q at root of unity q introduces a positive cosmological constant (Lambda ~ 1/k, so k->infinity recovers classical SU(2) and Lambda->0), renders amplitudes IR-finite, and yields curved-4-simplex Regge asymptotics. NOTE: the precise numerical coefficient Lambda = 6 pi/(l_P^2 k) was not independently verified; only the Lambda ~ 1/k scaling is confirmed.

references.title: New Variables for Classical and Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Introduced Ashtekar variables, founding canonical LQG by recasting GR as a gauge theory with polynomial constraints.

references.title: Loop space representation of quantum general relativity
references.significance: Established the loop representation, the birth of non-perturbative quantum gravity.

references.title: Discreteness of area and volume in quantum gravity
references.significance: Derived the discrete area and volume spectra, the signature prediction of quantized geometry.

references.title: Spin networks and quantum gravity
references.significance: Identified spin networks as an orthonormal basis of the kinematical Hilbert space.

references.title: Quantum theory of geometry I: Area operators
references.significance: Rigorous construction of the area operator and its spectrum.

references.title: Quantum Spin Dynamics (QSD)
references.significance: First mathematically well-defined, anomaly-free Hamiltonian constraint operator.

references.authors: A. Ashtekar, J. Baez, A. Corichi, K. Krasnov
references.title: Quantum Geometry and Black Hole Entropy
references.significance: Derived black hole entropy from isolated-horizon microstate counting, introducing the role of gamma.

references.title: Black hole entropy from Quantum Geometry
references.significance: Precise U(1) microstate counting fixing the value of the Immirzi parameter.

references.title: Black hole entropy in Loop Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Established the universal -1/2 ln(A) logarithmic correction to the entropy.

references.authors: J. Lewandowski, A. Okolow, H. Sahlmann, T. Thiemann
references.title: Uniqueness of diffeomorphism invariant states on holonomy-flux algebras
references.significance: The LOST uniqueness theorem giving LQG kinematics its rigidity.

references.authors: A. Ashtekar, T. Pawlowski, P. Singh
references.title: Quantum Nature of the Big Bang
references.significance: Discovered the big bounce in LQC with critical density ~0.41 rho_Planck.

references.authors: E. Bianchi, L. Modesto, C. Rovelli, S. Speziale
references.title: Graviton propagator in loop quantum gravity
references.significance: First computation of the graviton two-point function from a spin foam, matching linearized gravity.

references.authors: J. Engle, R. Pereira, C. Rovelli, E. Livine
references.title: LQG vertex with finite Immirzi parameter
references.significance: Defined the EPRL vertex amplitude, the modern spin-foam model with finite gamma.

references.title: A new spin foam model for 4d gravity
references.significance: The FK model, complementary construction merged with EPRL into EPRL-FK.

references.authors: J. Engle, K. Noui, A. Perez
references.title: Black hole entropy and SU(2) Chern-Simons theory
references.significance: SU(2)-invariant horizon entropy and a manifestly gauge-invariant counting; the full SU(2) counting yields gamma ~ 0.274. (Note: arXiv 0905.3168 has exactly three authors; D. Pranzetti is NOT an author.)

references.title: Zakopane lectures on loop gravity
references.significance: Authoritative pedagogical definition of covariant LQG and the spin-foam transition amplitude.

references.title: A spin-foam vertex amplitude with the correct semiclassical limit
references.significance: Proper vertex isolating a single gravitational sector to suppress unwanted semiclassical terms.

references.title: The Spin Foam Approach to Quantum Gravity
references.significance: The standard Living Reviews survey of spin foams, EPRL model and open problems.

references.title: The twistorial structure of loop-gravity transition amplitudes
references.significance: Showed EPRL amplitudes derive from a twistor-space path integral, bridging LQG and twistor methods.

references.title: The Phoenix Project: Master Constraint Programme for Loop Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Proposed the master constraint to handle the non-closing Hamiltonian constraint algebra.

references.authors: M. Bojowald, H. A. Morales-Tecotl, H. Sahlmann
references.title: On Loop Quantum Gravity Phenomenology and the Issue of Lorentz Invariance
references.significance: Analysis of modified dispersion relations and Lorentz invariance in LQG phenomenology.

references.authors: H. Haggard, M. Han, W. Kaminski, A. Riello
references.title: SL(2,C) Chern-Simons theory, a non-planar graph operator, and 4D quantum gravity with a cosmological constant
references.significance: Incorporated a cosmological constant via quantum-group deformation with curved-4-simplex semiclassics.

references.title: Four-dimensional Spinfoam Quantum Gravity with Cosmological Constant: Finiteness and Semiclassical Limit
references.significance: Demonstrated finiteness and correct semiclassical limit of Lambda-deformed spin foams.

references.title: Loop Quantum Gravity, Exact Holographic Mapping, and Holographic Entanglement Entropy
references.significance: Mapped LQG spin networks to tensor networks satisfying the Ryu-Takayanagi formula.

references.authors: S. K. Asante, B. Dittrich, H. M. Haggard
references.title: Effective Spin Foam Models for Four-Dimensional Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Introduced computationally efficient effective spin foams diagnosing and avoiding the flatness problem.

references.authors: S. K. Asante, B. Dittrich, J. Padua-Arguelles
references.title: Effective Spin Foam Models for Lorentzian Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Lorentzian version of the effective spin foam models, enabling semiclassical tests in Lorentzian signature.

references.title: A high-performance code for EPRL spin foam amplitudes (sl2cfoam-next)
references.significance: Orders-of-magnitude speedup enabling numerical tests of asymptotics and the flatness problem.

references.authors: S. Gielen, D. Oriti, L. Sindoni
references.title: Cosmology from Group Field Theory Formalism for Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Derived Friedmann dynamics and LQC corrections from GFT condensates, bridging LQG and GFT.

references.authors: M. Bruno, E. Colafranceschi, F. M. Mele, C. Rovelli
references.title: The Structure of the Continuum Limit of Spin Foams
references.significance: TQFT/RAQ no-go and distributional rigging-map construction of the physical Hilbert space (2026).

references.authors: T. L. M. Guedes, G. A. Mena Marugan, F. Vidotto, M. Muller
references.title: Computing the graph-changing dynamics of loop quantum gravity
references.significance: First numerical implementation of the graph-changing Hamiltonian constraint.

references.title: Entanglement entropy in Loop Quantum Gravity and geometrical area law
references.significance: Derives a geometric area law for entanglement directly in LQG using von Neumann algebra methods.

references.title: Could Planck Star Remnants be Dark Matter?
references.significance: Proposes Planck-mass remnants from bounced black holes as a dark-matter candidate.

references.authors: R. P. Vyas, M. J. Joshi
references.title: The Barbero-Immirzi Parameter: An Enigmatic Parameter of Loop Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Review of the values, interpretations and open questions surrounding gamma (Physics 4(4), 1094-1116).

references.title: Covariant Loop Quantum Gravity
references.significance: Standard textbook on covariant LQG and spin foam theory.

references.authors: A. Eichhorn, B. Bahr, A. D. Pereira
references.title: Editorial: Coarse graining in quantum gravity - Bridging the gap between microscopic models and spacetime physics
references.significance: Surveys tensor-network renormalization of spin foams and its link to continuum/RG questions.

openProblems.statement: Define and solve the quantum Hamiltonian constraint (dynamics) of canonical LQG with a closing constraint algebra and a controlled physical state space.
openProblems.whyHard: Diffeomorphism invariance yields an algebra with structure functions (not constants); Thiemann's operator is graph-changing and plagued by factor-ordering and regularization ambiguities, and coherent states miss the newly generated degrees of freedom.
openProblems.attempts: Thiemann's QSD operator; the Master Constraint Programme; algebraic quantum gravity; 2024 numerical graph-changing implementations.

openProblems.statement: Prove that LQG / spin foams reproduce general relativity (plus controlled quantum corrections) in the semiclassical and continuum limits.
openProblems.whyHard: Background independence means there is no fixed background to perturb around; the large-spin and continuum limits need not commute, and strong convergence can collapse the theory to a topological one.
openProblems.attempts: Large-spin Regge asymptotics; graviton propagator; tensor-network/coarse-graining renormalization; 2026 distributional (TQFT/RAQ) continuum-limit framework.

openProblems.statement: Determine whether spin-foam dynamics is dominated by flat (Regge-flat) geometries in the semiclassical limit and how to avoid it.
openProblems.whyHard: Gluing constraints and the choice of areas as fundamental variables peak the path integral on flat configurations; the issue is confirmed numerically in BF and EPRL cases.
openProblems.attempts: Effective spin foams avoiding flatness in a restricted regime; numerical studies with sl2cfoam-next; Monte Carlo sampling.

openProblems.statement: Clarify the physical status and value of the Barbero-Immirzi parameter gamma.
openProblems.whyHard: Classically gamma is a canonical-transformation label (unobservable) yet quantum-mechanically sets the geometry scale and entropy; different counting schemes (U(1) vs SU(2), minimal-spin assumptions) give different values (~0.237-0.274).
openProblems.attempts: U(1) and SU(2) entropy countings; proposals making gamma a dynamical field; analytic-continuation (self-dual) approaches gamma = +-i.

openProblems.statement: Establish that the EPRL spin-foam amplitude implements the projector onto the kernel of the canonical LQG Hamiltonian constraint.
openProblems.whyHard: Canonical (graph-changing) and covariant (fixed 2-complex) formulations use different degrees of freedom; the equivalence requires controlling the sum/refinement over 2-complexes.
openProblems.attempts: Group field theory completion; refinement-limit and renormalization studies; boundary Hilbert space matching.

openProblems.statement: Incorporate matter (fermions, gauge fields) and recover Standard Model low-energy physics from LQG.
openProblems.whyHard: LQG quantizes geometry but has no built-in principle fixing the matter content; coupling matter to spin networks/foams and extracting particle physics remains piecemeal.
openProblems.attempts: Spin foams coupled to scalar/fermion fields; quantum-reduced loop gravity; speculative spinor/octonion Standard-Model constructions.

openProblems.statement: Show that discrete LQG geometry is compatible with (or testably deforms) local Lorentz invariance, and derive falsifiable phenomenology.
openProblems.whyHard: Discrete area is a frame-dependent-looking scale; reconciling it with relativity points to deformed (DSR/kappa-Poincare) rather than broken symmetry, but a full proof is lacking and data tightly constrain modified dispersions.
openProblems.attempts: Deformed hypersurface-deformation algebra; kappa-Poincare/DSR linkage; gamma-ray-burst dispersion constraints.

connections.description: GFT is the second-quantized reformulation of the LQG state space and a completion of spin foams: GFT Feynman amplitudes ARE spin foam amplitudes, and GFT condensates reproduce Friedmann dynamics with LQC-type bounce. The tightest, best-mapped bridge from LQG.

Loop quantum cosmology is the symmetry-reduced (homogeneous) sector of LQG; it yields the big bounce (rho_c ~ 0.41 rho_Pl), singularity resolution and CMB predictions. The most developed phenomenological application of LQG.

Both build spacetime from discrete simplicial/combinatorial data and use a sum-over-geometries. Key difference: LQG/spin foam discreteness is fundamental while CDT discreteness is a regularization removed by a continuum limit; effective spin foams as simplicial path integrals offer a bridge, but no shared phase-diagram dictionary exists.

Both seek a non-perturbative UV-complete gravity. Spin-foam coarse-graining/RG could in principle reach a non-trivial fixed point analogous to the asymptotic-safety scenario, identifying the continuum limit with a UV fixed point. No direct demonstration yet - a prime 'gold' bridge.

Spin networks are PEPS-type tensor networks with an entanglement area law; Han-Hung built an exact holographic mapping whose boundary entanglement satisfies the Ryu-Takayanagi formula in the semiclassical regime. A genuine LQG-CFT duality and full AdS/CFT dictionary are still missing.

LQG spin-network states realize a geometric entanglement area law (entropy = sum over boundary punctures), connecting quantized area to entanglement and to the 'geometry from entanglement' program; recent 2025 work derives the area law directly in LQG via von Neumann algebras.

The LQG phase space on a fixed graph decomposes into twistors (two per edge), with holonomies/fluxes as spinor bilinears and EPRL amplitudes derivable from a twistor-space path integral. The link to particle-physics spinor-helicity scattering amplitudes is suggested but computationally unexploited.

Linearizing the LQG deformed hypersurface-deformation algebra yields kappa-Poincare symmetry and a kappa-Minkowski noncommutative spacetime (DSR), suggesting deformed rather than broken Lorentz invariance and a route to QG phenomenology. Uniqueness of the implied deformation is unproven.

LQG reproduces Bekenstein-Hawking entropy via horizon microstate counting and offers bounce/Planck-star scenarios with information escaping the singularity; connections to the Page curve and unitary evaporation remain qualitative.

Recovering linearized gravity and the graviton propagator from spin foams, and the Regge action from large-spin vertex asymptotics, is the route by which LQG should reduce to semiclassical/effective gravity; the limit is partially demonstrated but not proven in general.

Historically rival programs: LQG is background-independent and predicts fundamental discreteness; string theory is background-dependent and perturbative. They share black-hole area-law entropy, discrete spectra and (recently) tensor-network/holographic structures. Possible but largely unexplored bridges via BF/topological structure or spin foams as worldsheet lattices.

Both posit fundamental spacetime discreteness with a discrete causal/combinatorial structure. Spin foams encode causal structure in the 2-complex while causal sets use partial orders; a precise translation between spin-foam histories and causal-set growth dynamics is essentially unexplored.

LQG predicts Planck-scale discreteness with potential modified dispersion relations (DSR), CMB imprints from the LQC bounce, and Planck-star/remnant dark-matter signatures - the main observational handles, all tightly constrained and not yet confirmed.

Black hole entropy proportional to horizon area emerges in LQG from counting punctures, an independent realization of the area-law / holographic-principle that strings realize via D-brane counting - a shared result reached by different microscopics.
